title: FAIL: corrupted Madelung order — k=4 and k=5 swapped
_fixture_purpose: Negative control. Same mapping and structural claim as the PASS fixture, but subshells k=4 and k=5 are swapped (3s before 2p instead of 2p before 3s). The QA (d, -e) sort independently regenerated by the validator will NOT match, tripping MAD_ORDER. Also trips MAD_RULE since the artificial (3,0) -> (2,1) transition violates both the antidiag rule (would require e=-1) and the jump rule (would require going backwards in d).
source_attribution: Will Dale + Claude 2026-04-13. Negative control for cert [220].

structural_claim.filling_order: QA (d, -e) ascending sort (this fixture has tampered sequence)

cross_references.note: negative control; PASS fixture is mad_pass_janet_36.json

fail_ledger.reason: k=4 should be (3,0) per QA (d,-e) sort but fixture has (3,1); k=5 should be (3,1) but fixture has (3,0)
transition k=4 -> k=5 goes from d=4 to d=3, violating non-decreasing d; not covered by within-d antidiag or between-d jump rule
